title: QUIC client-side 1-RTT Key Phase observation work item
summary: Add the first observed active-path 1-RTT Key Phase install boundary while keeping successor-key derivation, TLS KeyUpdate support, transfer/retry, and public API widening out of scope.
planned_changes:
- Record the first observed active-path 1-RTT Key Phase transition only after handshake confirmation.
- Keep establishing runtimes unchanged before handshake confirmation and avoid deriving successor keys or claiming TLS KeyUpdate support.
- Leave transfer, retry, and public API widening out of scope for this observation-only slice.
- Add focused requirement-home coverage for the observed transition boundary and the pre-confirmation negative case.
completion_notes: The managed client/runtime path now records the first observed 1-RTT Key Phase transition on the active short-header packet path after handshake confirmation, leaves establishing runtimes unchanged before handshake confirmation, and keeps successor-key derivation and public key-update support out of scope.
verification_plan: Run focused requirement-home tests for helper round-trip coverage, active-runtime first-transition install, establishing-runtime no-install behavior, fuzz coverage for the short-header Key Phase boundary, and the public no-broad-support guard; run the permanent BenchmarkDotNet suite for the 1-RTT Key Phase helper path; then run SpecTrace render and validation checks.
out_of_scope: opportunistic refactors unrelated to the Key Phase boundary
